expectations.text: Suggests features with clear user value
expectations.evidence: The sub-agent output format requires a 'Missing Features' table with columns: Feature, Priority (MUST/SHOULD/COULD), 'Why I need this' (in persona voice), and Spec section. The synthesis report then groups these into Recommended Actions (MUST/SHOULD/COULD) with rationale. SKILL.md enforces structured, value-linked suggestions by design.

expectations.text: Links suggestions to personas who need them
expectations.evidence: The synthesis template's 'Consensus Features' table includes a 'Suggested by' column listing persona names. The 'Feature Suggestions by Category' tables also include a 'Personas' column. Every suggestion carries persona attribution throughout the report.

Each agent's output format includes a 'Compliance/Standards Gaps' section with columns for Gap, Standard/Requirement (explicitly mentioning GEMMA, BIO2, NLGov, etc.), Impact level, and Recommendation. Agent 4 (Noor Yilmaz) focuses on BIO2/ENSIA/AVG; Agent 5 (Annemarie de Vries) focuses on GEMMA/Common Ground/NLGov.

expectations.text: Suggests OpenSpec changes for top features
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3 synthesis template includes a 'Potential OpenSpec Changes' section — a table of Change Name, Description, Related Personas, and Estimated Complexity — explicitly tied to '/opsx-new'. Step 4 also instructs: 'Offer to create OpenSpec changes for any of the suggested features'.

notes: The skill's structured output templates ensure every suggestion is specific, persona-attributed, compliance-aware, and actionable as an OpenSpec change. All four quality expectations are met by the skill's prescribed output format.
